problem_statement: How to safely embed generative AI/LLM assistants across the enterprise SDLC so feature delivery accelerates without sacrificing reliability, given that AI-generated code can be insecure or incorrect and needs human oversight and governance guardrails.
magnitude_evidence: engineers frequently spending roughly one-third of their time reading, categorizing, and routing issue tickets [1]
insufficiency_evidence: Prior work has shown that automatically suggested code may contain security vulnerabilities, underscoring the continued need for human oversight and robust validation [6], [8]
solution_mechanisms: I/O content filtering and PII redaction
progressive rollout with feature flags and blast-radius limits
LLM-based bug triage with human-approved auto-closure
self-hosted models for sensitive repos, non-learning cloud mode
large-suggestion scrutiny threshold and OSS near-copy detection
key_constructs: treat AI like a junior developer/contributor
false positives as human-overridden AI suggestions
